title: Markland POA Signage Project Application
description: Please provide comprehensive details about your signage project to ensure compliance with Markland POA Design Standards and Guidelines, and local sign ordinances. ALL SIGNAGE REQUIRES ARB APPROVAL BEFORE INSTALLATION.

primary.section: Section 7.15 Prohibited Sign Formats & Section 7.15.1 Real Estate Marketing Signs
primary.summary: Markland has extremely restrictive sign policies. Most signage is prohibited except for specific real estate marketing signs with exact specifications. The ARB has sole discretion to approve or deny any signage based on overall aesthetic impact to the community.
keyRequirements: ARB approval required for ALL signage before installation
Only ONE sign permitted per property for real estate marketing
Sign must be located ON the property for sale
10-foot minimum setback from edge of any roadway required
Specific material, color, and size specifications must be followed exactly
Most decorative, business, and personal signage is prohibited
Performance deposit required with ARB application
primary.quote: Only one sign shall be permitted for each property and the sign must be located on the property for sale. The sign shall be set back at least 10 feet from the edge of any roadway.

additionalReferences.summary: ARB has ultimate authority to approve or deny applications based on overall aesthetic impact, even if individual criteria are met
keyProvisions: ARB may waive specific provisions if overall aesthetic impact is acceptable
Approval of one application does not create obligation to approve similar designs

additionalReferences.summary: All improvements require formal ARB application process with performance deposit
keyProvisions: Completed Design Review Application Form required

fields.label: Primary Sign Type
options: Real Estate Marketing/For Sale Sign (APPROVED TYPE)
fields.description: Select the primary type of sign being installed. NOTE: Most sign types are prohibited or heavily restricted in Markland.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Only real estate marketing signs are explicitly permitted with specific design requirements
relevantBylaws.note: All other signage types require special ARB approval and most will likely be denied
relevantBylaws.quote: Real Estate Marketing and For Sale Signs - Only one sign shall be permitted for each property

fields.label: Sign Purpose and Content
fields.placeholder: For real estate marketing only, or detailed justification for other signage types
fields.description: Describe the purpose of the sign and what information it will display. If not real estate marketing, provide detailed justification for necessity.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Must demonstrate purpose aligns with community aesthetic standards
relevantBylaws.note: ARB evaluates total effect and aesthetic impact on community

fields.label: Sign Text Content
fields.placeholder:
FOR SALE
[Agent Name]
[Phone Number]
fields.description: Provide the exact text that will appear on the sign. For real estate signs, text must be minimal and professional.

fields.label: Graphics, Logos, or Symbols
fields.placeholder: Real estate company logo only (for approved real estate signs)
fields.description: Describe any graphics, logos, or symbols. Excessive graphics may be prohibited.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Specific logo and lettering color requirements must be followed
relevantBylaws.note: Logo: PMC 548C, Border: PMC 548C, Other lettering: Black

fields.label: Temporary or Permanent Installation
fields.description: Most permanent signage is prohibited in Markland except real estate marketing during active sales.

fields.label: Temporary Sign Duration
fields.placeholder: During active real estate listing only
fields.description: If temporary, specify the expected duration. Real estate signs only permitted during active listings.

fields.label: Sign Dimensions
fields.placeholder:
Width: 18 inches
Height: 12 inches
Thickness: As specified
Total area: Must comply with Markland specifications
fields.description: Provide detailed sign dimensions. Real estate signs have specific size requirements per Markland specifications.

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.15.1 Real Estate Marketing Signs Exhibit
relevantBylaws.requirement: Specific dimensions required: 12"X18" Sign with 4"X4" PT Post and 2"X4"X24" PT Support arm
relevantBylaws.note: Exact specifications must be followed for approved sign types

fields.label: Sign Materials
options: Wood with PT posts (APPROVED for real estate signs)
fields.description: Real estate signs must use specified materials: 4"X4" PT Post with decorative dado.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Materials specified: 4"X4" PT Post with decorative dado, 2"X4"X24" PT Support arm rabbit jointed
relevantBylaws.note: Exact material specifications must be followed

fields.label: Material Specifications
fields.placeholder:
4"X4" Pressure-treated post with decorative dado
2"X4"X24" PT Support arm rabbit jointed
12"X18" Sign panel
fields.description: Must follow exact Markland specifications for approved sign types.

fields.label: Sign Colors
fields.placeholder:
Post: PMS 548C
Sign: White
Logo: PMC 548C
Border: PMC 548C
Other lettering: Black
Decorative dado: White
fields.description: Real estate signs must use exact color specifications. All colors must be ARB approved.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Exact color specifications: Post: PMS 548C, Sign: White, Logo: PMC 548C, Border: PMC 548C, Other lettering: Black, Decorative dado: White
relevantBylaws.note: No deviations from specified colors allowed for real estate signs

fields.label: Lettering Style and Font
fields.placeholder:
Font: Goudy Old Style Bold (for real estate signs)
Size: As specified in Markland guidelines
Color: Black lettering
fields.description: Real estate signs must use Goudy Old Style Bold font as specified.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Font: Goudy Old Style Bold
relevantBylaws.note: Exact font specification must be followed for approved signs

fields.label: Sign Illumination
fields.description: Illuminated signage is generally prohibited or heavily restricted in Markland.

fields.label: Illumination Details
fields.placeholder: Must comply with Section 7.16 Lighting requirements if approved
fields.description: Any lighting must comply with Markland lighting standards and requires ARB approval.

fields.label: Markland Lot Type Classification
fields.description: Lot type affects signage restrictions and setback requirements.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Different lot types may have specific signage restrictions
relevantBylaws.note: Corner lots and special lot conditions may have additional requirements

fields.label: Sign Location on Property
fields.placeholder: Front yard, oriented parallel to primary road, minimum 10 feet from roadway edge, on property for sale...
fields.description: Must comply with Markland setback and orientation requirements.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Sign must be located on the property for sale, set back at least 10 feet from edge of roadway, oriented parallel to primary road if one sign face used
relevantBylaws.note: Setback requirements strictly enforced

fields.label: Mounting Method
options: Post-mounted per Markland specifications (APPROVED for real estate)
fields.description: Real estate signs must use specified post-mounting method.

fields.label: Mounting Specifications
fields.placeholder: 4"X4" Pressure-treated post, 2"X4"X24" PT Support arm rabbit jointed, 5' off grade per Markland specifications
fields.description: Must follow exact mounting specifications from Markland Design Standards.

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.15.1 Real Estate Marketing Signs Exhibit
relevantBylaws.requirement: Specific mounting: 4"X4" PT Post with decorative dado, 2"X4"X24" PT Support arm rabbit jointed, 5' OFF GRADE
relevantBylaws.note: Exact specifications must be followed

fields.label: Sign Height Above Ground
fields.placeholder: 5 feet off grade (per Markland specifications)
fields.description: Real estate signs must be 5 feet off grade per Markland specifications.

fields.label: Setback Distances
fields.placeholder:
Minimum 10 feet from edge of roadway (REQUIRED)
Compliance with all property line setbacks
No encroachment into easements
fields.description: Must comply with Markland minimum setback requirements and not encroach into easements.

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.15.1 Real Estate Marketing Signs & Section 4.7 Easements
relevantBylaws.requirement: Minimum 10-foot setback from roadway edge required, no encroachment into easements permitted
relevantBylaws.note: Setback violations will result in immediate removal requirement

fields.label: Utility and Easement Clearance
options: No utilities or easements in sign area
Utilities located and sign placement avoids conflicts
fields.description: Must avoid all utility easements and underground utilities.

relevantBylaws.requirement: No encroachment upon easements permitted, ARB approval required for any improvements within easements
relevantBylaws.note: FPL electrical easements occur on front of all lots along property line

fields.label: ARB Application Status
fields.description: ARB approval is MANDATORY before any sign installation in Markland.

relevantBylaws.requirement: No construction of any kind shall commence until applicants receive their final approval
relevantBylaws.note: Violations result in immediate stop-work orders and potential fines

fields.label: Performance Deposit Status
fields.description: Refundable performance deposit required with ARB application.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Refundable Performance Deposit required, refundable at successful completion and compliance with Design Standards
relevantBylaws.note: Deposit forfeited if work not brought into compliance within 90 days

fields.label: Design Review Application Form
options: Form completed and ready for submission
fields.description: Completed Design Review Application Form required from ARB.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Completed Design Review Application Form required, unsigned applications considered incomplete
relevantBylaws.note: Forms provided by ARB upon request

fields.label: ARB Meeting Coordination
fields.placeholder: Meeting date scheduled, submission deadline confirmed
fields.description: Coordinate with ARB for review meeting if required.

fields.label: Compliance Inspection Plan
fields.placeholder:
Will notify ARB when installation complete for final inspection
Understand inspection required before deposit refund
fields.description: Final compliance inspection required before performance deposit refund.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Prior to issuance of refundable performance deposit, all requirements will be inspected for compliance
relevantBylaws.note: Applicant must notify when ready for inspection

fields.label: Markland Design Standards Compliance
options: Sign fully complies with all Markland specifications
Sign requires ARB approval for variations
fields.description: Compliance with Markland Design Standards and Guidelines is mandatory.

fields.label: St. Johns County Sign Ordinance Compliance
options: Sign complies with all St. Johns County ordinances
fields.description: Must also comply with St. Johns County sign ordinances in addition to Markland requirements.

relevantBylaws.requirement: All new construction must conform to applicable governmental codes, ordinances, regulations, permits and laws
relevantBylaws.note: Both Markland and county requirements must be met

fields.label: Required Permits and Approvals
fields.description: Markland ARB approval is always required, additional permits may be needed.

fields.label: Permit Details and Status
fields.placeholder: ARB Application #[number], County permit #[number] if applicable
fields.description: Provide permit numbers and status for all required approvals.

fields.label: Business/Commercial Sign Restrictions
fields.placeholder: Home business signs generally prohibited in Markland residential community
fields.description: Business signage is generally prohibited in Markland residential areas.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Most business and commercial signage prohibited in residential community
relevantBylaws.note: Home occupation signage typically not permitted

fields.label: Installation Method
fields.description: Installation must follow exact Markland specifications regardless of installer.

fields.label: Installer Information and Licensing
fields.placeholder: Installer name and license if using professional service
fields.description: Professional installer licensing may be required for certain sign types.

fields.label: Construction Hours Compliance
options: Will comply with St. Johns County construction hours
Will follow any additional Markland time restrictions
fields.description: Must comply with construction hour restrictions.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Construction hours shall generally comply with St. Johns County regulations, Developer and Association reserve right to limit weekend/holiday hours
relevantBylaws.note: Additional restrictions may apply as residents occupy community

fields.label: Installation Timeline
fields.placeholder:
Installation date: [date]
Duration: [time]
Work hours: Comply with Markland construction hours
ARB approval received: [date]
fields.description: Installation timeline must account for ARB approval process and construction hour restrictions.

fields.label: Site Preparation and Access
fields.placeholder:
Minimal site disturbance
Utility marking completed
No damage to common area improvements
Access coordinated to avoid disruption
fields.description: Site preparation must minimize impact and avoid damage to common areas.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Must coordinate to avoid disruption to common area improvements, responsible for maintaining clean construction site
relevantBylaws.note: Owner/Builder responsible for protection/replacement of damaged common area enhancements

fields.label: Site Cleanup and Maintenance Plan
fields.placeholder:
Immediate cleanup of installation area
Removal of all debris and materials
Daily cleanup during installation
No debris left on adjacent properties
fields.description: Must maintain clean site and remove all debris immediately.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Maintain clean and orderly construction site, clean any debris from neighborhood streets, trash must be picked up every day
relevantBylaws.note: Debris and construction spillage must be cleaned from community streets

fields.label: Markland Community Character Compatibility
fields.placeholder: Sign design maintains Markland's high-quality residential character, follows approved specifications exactly...
fields.description: Sign must maintain Markland's distinctive community character and aesthetic standards.

relevantBylaws.requirement: ARB evaluates each application for total effect and overall aesthetic impact, decisions based on matters of judgment and taste
relevantBylaws.note: Sign might meet individual criteria but still be denied if overall aesthetic impact unacceptable

fields.label: Architectural Harmony Assessment
fields.placeholder: Sign complements approved architectural styles in Markland, maintains quality standards...
fields.description: Sign must harmonize with Markland's approved architectural character.

fields.label: Visibility and Traffic Safety
fields.placeholder: Sign placement ensures clear sight lines, appropriate height for safety, no traffic obstruction...
fields.description: Sign must not create safety hazards or obstruct traffic visibility.

fields.label: Neighbor Impact Assessment
fields.placeholder: Sign maintains community standards, no negative impact on adjacent properties, professional appearance...
fields.description: Sign must not negatively impact neighboring properties or community standards.

fields.label: Neighbor Communication
options: No neighbor notification needed for compliant signs
fields.description: Consider neighbor impact, especially for non-standard signage requests.

fields.label: Weather Resistance and Durability
fields.description: Must use weather-resistant materials specified in Markland guidelines.

fields.label: Sign Maintenance Schedule
fields.placeholder:
Regular inspection and maintenance
Prompt repair of any damage
Replacement when worn or non-compliant
Removal when no longer needed (real estate signs)
fields.description: Ongoing maintenance required to maintain compliance with Markland standards.

fields.label: Ongoing Compliance Monitoring
options: Will promptly address any compliance issues
Agree to remove sign if becomes non-compliant
Will coordinate with ARB for any modifications
fields.description: Ongoing compliance with Markland standards is required.

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 2.6 Design Standards and Guidelines Checklist
relevantBylaws.requirement: If work deemed not in compliance, correspondence issued with 90 days to remedy, failure results in deposit retention
relevantBylaws.note: Ongoing compliance monitoring and enforcement by ARB

fields.label: Sign Removal Plan
fields.placeholder:
For real estate signs: immediate removal when property sale complete
For other signs: removal when no longer needed or if required by ARB
Complete site restoration upon removal
fields.description: Plan for eventual sign removal and site restoration.

relevantBylaws.requirement: Real estate signs must be removed promptly when property is no longer for sale
relevantBylaws.note: Temporary signs must be removed when purpose fulfilled

criticalReminders: ARB APPROVAL IS MANDATORY BEFORE ANY SIGN INSTALLATION - No exceptions
Most signage types are PROHIBITED in Markland except real estate marketing signs
Real estate signs must follow EXACT specifications - no deviations allowed
Minimum 10-foot setback from roadway edge required for all signage
Performance deposit required and refundable only after compliance inspection
90-day compliance deadline - non-compliance results in deposit forfeiture
Must comply with both Markland standards AND St. Johns County ordinances
commonViolations: Installing signs without ARB approval (immediate violation)
Using non-approved materials, colors, or fonts for real estate signs
Incorrect setback distances from roadways or property lines
Business or commercial signage in residential areas (prohibited)
Signs that encroach into utility or drainage easements
Failing to remove temporary signs when no longer needed
Multiple signs per property (only one real estate sign allowed)
Decorative or artistic signage without special approval

required_documents: Completed Markland Design Review Application Form (from ARB)
Sign design plans showing exact specifications per Markland guidelines
Material specifications matching approved Markland requirements
Installation plans with mounting details per specifications
Site plan showing sign location with accurate setback measurements
St. Johns County sign permits (if required)
Professional installer license and insurance (if applicable)
Color samples and material samples (if deviating from standard)
ARB approval letter before installation begins

arbApplicationProcess.step1: Contact ARB to obtain Design Review Application Form
arbApplicationProcess.step2: Complete application with all required documentation and plans
arbApplicationProcess.step3: Submit refundable performance deposit with application
arbApplicationProcess.step4: Wait for ARB review and decision (allow adequate time)
arbApplicationProcess.step5: Receive written approval before beginning any work
arbApplicationProcess.step6: Install sign exactly per approved specifications
arbApplicationProcess.step7: Notify ARB when installation complete for inspection
arbApplicationProcess.step8: Pass compliance inspection to receive deposit refund
arbApplicationProcess.timeline: Allow minimum 2-4 weeks for ARB review process
arbApplicationProcess.contact: Contact ARB for current submittal deadlines and meeting schedules

cornerLots.requirements: Corner lots may have additional aesthetic requirements for street-facing facades
cornerLots.note: Side facades facing streets must meet higher design standards

lakefrontLots.requirements: Special landscape and aesthetic requirements may affect signage placement
lakefrontLots.note: Filtered views and landscape screening requirements may restrict signage

traditionalLots.requirements: Traditional lots may have specific design character requirements
traditionalLots.note: Signage must complement traditional architectural character

perimeterLots.requirements: Perimeter lots may have additional screening and aesthetic requirements
perimeterLots.note: Enhanced architectural standards may affect signage approval
